dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, 307-B, breathing problem for a 68-year-old female at 1412 W Broad Street.
Good afternoon ma'am, I'm John, an EMT with Med Minute Rescue. What made you call 911 today?
I've been having trouble breathing, and it feels like a COPD flare-up started about an hour ago.
Can you tell me your name please?
My name is Christine. I'm 68 years old.
Cool. Where are we, Christine?
We are in Richmond, in my living room.
Let me quickly check your airway. Is that okay with you?
How would you describe your breathing lately?
She seems alert and oriented. Let me check her circulation and vitals now.
Her heart rate is 123 beats per minute right now.
Respirations are 21 per minute, and she's breathing uncontrollably.
Blood pressure is 171 over 94.
Oxygen saturation is 98%, which is good for now.
EKG was not clear initially and monitoring will continue.
When did the trouble breathing start, and is anything making it better or worse?
It started an hour ago. I have COPD and hypertension. I haven't had anything make it better.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate the breathing difficulty?
It's a 5. I can't speak in full sentences because of how bad I'm breathing.
Let me do a quick mental status check. Are you aware of the time and what day it is today?
Skin is pink, warm, dry, and turgor is normal. No signs of rashes.
Pupils are reactive and equal, and I don't see any swelling or redness in the neck.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Partner
dialogue.utterance: No drainage or abnormalities found in the oral cavity.
Breath sounds are diminished in the lower lobes with some wheezing.
I did use 8 puffs of my albuterol and increased my oxygen to 4 LPM before you came.
We'll place you on ETCO2 nasal cannula to monitor your breathing continuously. It should be comfortable.
Administering 0.25 mg of Terbutaline via subcutaneous in your left arm now.
I attempted an IV in the left and right arms, but was unsuccessful due to difficulty accessing the vein.
I attempted IV access again in both arms but was still unsuccessful.
We've obtained a 4-lead and 12-lead ECG. Initial interpretation is sinus tachycardia.
I checked your glucose level earlier. It was 0, but you said you don't have a history of diabetes.
You mentioned back pain. Any issues with movement or sensation in the extremities?
Some back discomfort, but my arms and legs feel fine.
Her breathing has improved-rate is now 12 per minute.
Saturations are up to 99% and her BP is 156 over 111.
She seems more stable now after the Terbutaline.
We are following protocol for COPD exacerbation and respiratory distress. You're responding positively to treatment.
Per you, we are taking you to VCU Medical Center, correct?
We'll assist you with a safe transfer from your couch to the stretcher. Will you need help standing or prefer to be assisted to sit?
I can help myself up a little if you push the stretcher up.
Great. I'll help you move from the couch to the stretcher now.
She's now on the stretcher and ready for transport.
The stretcher is secured to the ambulance. We'll be en route to VCU Medical Center.
Upon arrival, you'll be moved to the hospital bed using a sheet transfer. Any concerns or preferences you'd like to share?
No, you're doing a good job. Thank you.
